matches.x: AND UNLEAVENED…CAKES. They were thick, as wafers13Hebrew, rekike. Rekike is connected to the word rek (empty, poor, or thin) (Krinsky). See rakkot basar (lean-fleshed) (Gen. 41:19). Thus rekike matzot (wafers unleavened) means thin unleavened bread. unleavened proves.14The wafers stand in contrast to the bread and cakes. Thus if the wafers are thin, the bread and cakes must be thick.
